page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing 96.0 GB of system memory. The CPU architecture is arm64, featuring 36 MB of L3 cache and hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8gn.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, achieving uncached read speeds of 402.56 GB/sec and write speeds of 198.42 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as evidenced by top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core tests, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark database operations. Static web serving throughput reaches 101.03 GB/sec. However, single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the average tier, and text generation for small-scale language models is relatively weak.

The ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads, offering high memory bandwidth and network throughput without the overhead of x86 licensing. The baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps supports intensive data transfers. Tradeoffs include the absence of local NVMe storage and lack of x86-specific vector instructions. This server is optimized for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. Equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory and a Nitro hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core capabilities, particularly in software compilation, database operations, and uncached memory bandwidth, which reaches 402.56 GB/sec for reads. While single-core performance and small-model text generation are average to weak, the server's high multi-threaded efficiency and memory bandwidth make it highly qualitative for concurrent workloads. It is optimized for static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, software building, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 48 Graviton physical cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 Graviton cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering high multi-core efficiency and exceptional memory bandwidth for network-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory with top-tier uncached read and write bandwidth
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and data transfer
Top-tier performance in software compilation, database operations, and web serving
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and medium LLM inference
